Legacy Goal: To digitalise all municipal land and services (e-governance)

To make Kitwe a clean city through (i) improvement of Solid Waste Management (SWM), and (ii) decongestion of the city by increasing the capacity of bus stations
To make Lusaka beautiful and climate smart city by the year 2025
To develop a climate, clean, green city: To plant an additional 3000 trees in the city
To reduce youth unemployment by 25% (122,000 young people) by 2026, by creating 30,000 quality and decent jobs for young people
To position Zanzibar to become a leading Blue Economy City, by 2025, by investing in waste collection and waste conversion
To improve water supply, provide service delivery through improved sanitation, and improve  markets through supporting informal vendors by 2028.
Creating 120,000 dignified jobs for women, youth and children in agriculture, digital tech, textiles, and finance.
Creating equitable access to education for 200,000 learners in Kajiado by 2027
Improved Solid Waste Management Care for the environment and working towards cleanliness Prevention of communicable diseases Improvement of groundwater
Improving Ndola’s  transit hub by creating an inter-city bus terminus and improving accessibility for all
Preserving coastal zones by tackling flood issues and improving communities impacted by climate change.

Provision of Communal Sanitation and Water Services to Windhoek’s 54945 informal settlement households in line with these basic services to Informal Settlements Plan 2019-2027 in order to combat urban decay and restore dignity to City of Windhoek’s residents.

Empowering 5000 women and girls in Banjul by expanding access to essential services, promoting economic inclusion, and opening pathways to civic and political leadership; ensuring meaningful participation in the city’s social and economic transformation by 2027
Improve water quality and service delivery by upgrading 50% of sewer system within 4 years
To reclaim, restore  and rebuild the  inner city of  Johannesburg (city  centre) by 2026

Empowering youth to launch and grow businesses, while connecting them to lucrative market opportunities during peak tourist seasons, generating sustainable employment and revenue to support the revitalization of youth-focused infrastructure.

To make Kasama a movement-friendly and safer city for all by improving the existing condition of 100 km of urban roads in our city by 2026 to offer conducive movement for vehicles, the physically challenged, school going children, pedestrians, motorcycle riders and bicycle riders; street vendors are also to have demarcation provisions within these upgrades.
To make Keur Massar Nord the greenest city in Senegal.

Quench Mombasa’s thirst and ensure no child misses school due to lack of water by increasing household water connections from 20,000 to 120,000 out of the County’s 350,000 households over the next two years. This will be achieved by: (1) reducing non-revenue water by half — from 60% to 30% — within the same period; and (2) concluding a hydrogeological survey to explore and sustainably exploit a deep aquifer, increasing daily supply from the current 20,000 cubic meters to 100,000 cubic meters, against a total demand of 200,000 cubic meters
Bringing together our communities, private sector, donors, and government, we will undertake transformative initiatives that make Monrovia the cleanest city in the Mano River Region.
To establish Shaggar as a premier economic powerhouse and livable smart city in Africa by 2032
To make Arba Minch a city of choice for all and amongest top 10 tourist destinations in Africa by realizing 50 public spaces, 25,000 dignified houses, and 40,000 decent jobs by 2030.
Making Bahir Dar a peerless African waterfront city and an international tourist destination

To make Bishoftu an international tourist destination: Through pursuing advanced city branding, development of 36 public and private tourism sites, building 67 km² of people-centered (road, cycling, walking) infrastructures to attract and serve 15 million domestic and international visitors annually by 2030.
To reduce youth unemployment by 40% and build a modern urban infrastructure system.

To create 300,000 jobs by 2030 for the most vulnerable youth through skills training, disbursement and monitoring of City Government loans, supplemented by National Government, development partners and the private sector

To deliver a modern Electric Bus Mass Transit system and advance the Light Rail Transit (LRT) vision that will reshape how Accra moves — enabling faster commutes, reducing pollution, supporting economic growth, and demonstrating that African cities can lead the global urban mobility transition.

To institutionalise a full-time Investment Delivery and Economic Impact system within TEDA that accelerates catalytic projects and embeds structured, data-driven performance and impact measurement aligned to the Tshwane Economic Revitalisation Strategy (TERS) and the City’s 3.9% economic growth target.
Formalisation and upgrading of informal setlements in Khomas Region

Improving the quality of life, through a coherent public health policy, based on effective waste management, environmental protection and the empowerment of local communities.
